heading: Life-cycle management and product support
label: 4324 Life-cycle management and product support

children.text: that is approved by the service acquisition executive responsible for such covered system; and
that meets applicable operational readiness requirements and materiel readiness objectives (established under ) in the most cost-effective manner practicable.section 118(c) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: to best achieve operational readiness requirements and materiel readiness objectives throughout the planned life cycle of such system; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that allows evaluation and selection of product support integrators and product support providers appropriate to execute the product support strategy.

children.label: i
children.text: is based on the product support strategy described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the covered system is a major defense acquisition program or major subprogram, is developed in accordance with the requirements to support a Milestone A approval, Milestone B approval, or Milestone C approval.

children.label: C
children.text: Recommended engineering and design considerations that support cost-effective sustainment of the covered system and best value solutions in life cycle planning and management.

children.label: D
children.text: An intellectual property management plan for product support developed in accordance with , including requirements for technical data, software, and modular open system approaches (as defined in ).section 3774 of this titlesection 4401 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: A strategy to maximize use of public and private sector capabilities including public-private partnerships, when appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: a description of the necessary tooling or other unique support equipment, requirements for initial spare parts and components, technical handbooks and maintenance manuals, maintenance training, and facilities;

children.label: ii
children.text: an identification of the funding required to provide such products and services for any initial fielding location of the covered system; and

children.label: iii
children.text: an assessment of the required number of training simulators, including the initial operational capability and overall fielding of such simulators.

children.label: A
children.text: consider affordability constraints and key cost factors that could affect operating and support costs during the life cycle of the covered system;

children.label: B
children.text: consider sustainment risks or challenges to sustaining the covered system in operational environments, including contested logistics environments (as defined in );section 2926 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: requirements to maintain a core logistics capability under ; andsection 2464 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: limitations on the performance of depot-level maintenance of materiel under ;section 2466 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: seek to integrate commercial best practices, use commercial standards, and use advanced technologies to enhance the product support of each covered system; and

children.label: E
children.text: seek to maintain a robust, resilient, and innovative defense industrial base to support requirements throughout the life cycle of the covered system.

children.heading: .—1So in original. No subsec. (c) has been enacted.1Definitions
children.label: d .—1So in original. No subsec. (c) has been enacted.1Definitions

children.text: The terms “milestone decision authority” and “Milestone A approval” have the meanings given, respectively, in .section 4251 of this title
The term “Milestone B approval” and “Milestone C approval” has the meaning given in .section 4172 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a major defense acquisition program as defined in ;section 4201 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a major subprogram as described in ; orsection 4203 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: an acquisition program or project that is carried out using the rapid fielding or rapid prototyping acquisition pathway under that is estimated by the Secretary of Defense to require an eventual total expenditure described in .section 3602 of this titlesection 4201(a)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: parts and systems designated as Mission Impaired Capability Awaiting Parts, Not Mission Capable Supply, or Casualty Report Category 3 or 4 status (as defined by the Secretary of Defense or a Secretary of a military department); or

children.label: B
children.text: parts or systems designated by the Secretary of Defense as negatively impacting the materiel readiness objectives of a covered system.

children.label: A
children.text: product support management;

children.label: B
children.text: design interface;

children.label: C
children.text: sustaining engineering;

children.label: D
children.text: maintenance planning and management;

children.label: E
children.text: supply support;

children.label: F
children.text: support equipment;

children.label: G
children.text: technical data;

children.label: H
children.text: training and training support;

children.label: I
children.text: information technology systems continuous support;

children.label: J
children.text: facilities and infrastructure;

children.label: K
children.text: packaging, handling, storage, and transportation; and

children.label: L
children.text: manpower and personnel.

The term “product support” means the package of support functions required to field and sustain the readiness and operational capability of covered systems (including subsystems and components of such covered systems).

children.label: A
children.text: means a contract, task order, or another contractual arrangement, or any type of agreement or non-contractual arrangement entered into by the Federal Government, for the performance of sustainment or logistics support required for covered systems (including subsystems and components of such covered systems); and

children.label: i
children.text: Performance-based logistics.

children.label: ii
children.text: Sustainment support.

children.label: iii
children.text: Contractor logistics support.

children.label: iv
children.text: Life-cycle product support.
The term “product support integrator” means an entity responsible for integrating private and public sources of product support within the scope of a product support arrangement.
The term “product support provider” means an entity that provides product support functions.
